{
  "markdown": "Publishing a Crate to Crates.io - The Rust Programming Language\n\n## Keyboard shortcuts\n\nPress ← or → to navigate between chapters\n\nPress S or / to search in the book\n\nPress ? to show this help\n\nPress Esc to hide this help\n\n- Auto\n- Light\n- Rust\n- Coal\n- Navy\n- Ayu\n\n# The Rust Programming Language\n\n## Publishing a Crate to Crates.io\n\nWe’ve used packages from [crates.io](https://crates.io/) as dependencies of our project, but you can also share your code with other people by publishing your own packages. The crate registry at [crates.io](https://crates.io/) distributes the source code of your packages, so it primarily hosts code that is open source.\n\nRust and Cargo have features that make your published package easier for people to find and use. We’ll talk about some of these features next and then explain how to publish a package.\n\n### Making Useful Documentation Comments\n\nAccurately documenting your packages will help other users know how and when to use them, so it’s worth investing the time to write documentation. In Chapter 3, we discussed how to comment Rust code using two slashes,&#96;//&#96;. Rust also has a particular kind of comment for documentation, known conveniently as a documentation comment, that will generate HTML documentation. The HTML displays the contents of documentation comments for public API items intended for programmers interested in knowing how to use your crate as opposed to how your crate is implemented.\n\nDocumentation comments use three slashes,&#96;///&#96;, instead of two and support Markdown notation for formatting the text. Place documentation comments just before the item they’re documenting. Listing 14-1 shows documentation comments for an&#96;add_one&#96; function in a crate named&#96;my_crate&#96;.\n\nFilename: src/lib.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/// Adds one to the number given.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// &#96;&#96;&#96;\n/// let arg = 5;\n/// let answer = my_crate::add_one(arg);\n///\n/// assert_eq!(6, answer);\n/// &#96;&#96;&#96;\npub fn add_one(x: i32) -\u003e i32 {\n    x + 1\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-1: A documentation comment for a function\n\nHere, we give a description of what the&#96;add_one&#96; function does, start a section with the heading&#96;Examples&#96;, and then provide code that demonstrates how to use the&#96;add_one&#96; function. We can generate the HTML documentation from this documentation comment by running&#96;cargo doc&#96;. This command runs the&#96;rustdoc&#96; tool distributed with Rust and puts the generated HTML documentation in the target/doc directory.\n\nFor convenience, running&#96;cargo doc --open&#96; will build the HTML for your current crate’s documentation (as well as the documentation for all of your crate’s dependencies) and open the result in a web browser. Navigate to the&#96;add_one&#96; function and you’ll see how the text in the documentation comments is rendered, as shown in Figure 14-1.\n\nFigure 14-1: The HTML documentation for the&#96;add_one&#96; function\n\n#### Commonly Used Sections\n\nWe used the&#96;# Examples&#96; Markdown heading in Listing 14-1 to create a section in the HTML with the title “Examples.” Here are some other sections that crate authors commonly use in their documentation:\n\n- Panics: These are the scenarios in which the function being documented could panic. Callers of the function who don’t want their programs to panic should make sure they don’t call the function in these situations.\n- Errors: If the function returns a&#96;Result&#96;, describing the kinds of errors that might occur and what conditions might cause those errors to be returned can be helpful to callers so that they can write code to handle the different kinds of errors in different ways.\n- Safety: If the function is&#96;unsafe&#96; to call (we discuss unsafety in Chapter 20), there should be a section explaining why the function is unsafe and covering the invariants that the function expects callers to uphold.\n\nMost documentation comments don’t need all of these sections, but this is a good checklist to remind you of the aspects of your code users will be interested in knowing about.\n\n#### Documentation Comments as Tests\n\nAdding example code blocks in your documentation comments can help demonstrate how to use your library and has an additional bonus: Running&#96;cargo test&#96; will run the code examples in your documentation as tests! Nothing is better than documentation with examples. But nothing is worse than examples that don’t work because the code has changed since the documentation was written. If we run&#96;cargo test&#96; with the documentation for the&#96;add_one&#96; function from Listing 14-1, we will see a section in the test results that looks like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n   Doc-tests my_crate\n\nrunning 1 test\ntest src/lib.rs - add_one (line 5) ... ok\n\ntest result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.27s\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNow, if we change either the function or the example so that the&#96;assert_eq!&#96; in the example panics, and run&#96;cargo test&#96; again, we’ll see that the doc tests catch that the example and the code are out of sync with each other!\n\n#### Contained Item Comments\n\nThe style of doc comment&#96;//!&#96; adds documentation to the item that contains the comments rather than to the items following the comments. We typically use these doc comments inside the crate root file (src/lib.rs by convention) or inside a module to document the crate or the module as a whole.\n\nFor example, to add documentation that describes the purpose of the&#96;my_crate&#96; crate that contains the&#96;add_one&#96; function, we add documentation comments that start with&#96;//!&#96; to the beginning of the src/lib.rs file, as shown in Listing 14-2.\n\nFilename: src/lib.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n//! # My Crate\n//!\n//! &#96;my_crate&#96; is a collection of utilities to make performing certain\n//! calculations more convenient.\n\n/// Adds one to the number given.\n// --snip--\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// &#96;&#96;&#96;\n/// let arg = 5;\n/// let answer = my_crate::add_one(arg);\n///\n/// assert_eq!(6, answer);\n/// &#96;&#96;&#96;\npub fn add_one(x: i32) -\u003e i32 {\n    x + 1\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-2: The documentation for the&#96;my_crate&#96; crate as a whole\n\nNotice there isn’t any code after the last line that begins with&#96;//!&#96;. Because we started the comments with&#96;//!&#96; instead of&#96;///&#96;, we’re documenting the item that contains this comment rather than an item that follows this comment. In this case, that item is the src/lib.rs file, which is the crate root. These comments describe the entire crate.\n\nWhen we run&#96;cargo doc --open&#96;, these comments will display on the front page of the documentation for&#96;my_crate&#96; above the list of public items in the crate, as shown in Figure 14-2.\n\nDocumentation comments within items are useful for describing crates and modules especially. Use them to explain the overall purpose of the container to help your users understand the crate’s organization.\n\nFigure 14-2: The rendered documentation for&#96;my_crate&#96;, including the comment describing the crate as a whole\n\n### Exporting a Convenient Public API\n\nThe structure of your public API is a major consideration when publishing a crate. People who use your crate are less familiar with the structure than you are and might have difficulty finding the pieces they want to use if your crate has a large module hierarchy.\n\nIn Chapter 7, we covered how to make items public using the&#96;pub&#96; keyword, and how to bring items into a scope with the&#96;use&#96; keyword. However, the structure that makes sense to you while you’re developing a crate might not be very convenient for your users. You might want to organize your structs in a hierarchy containing multiple levels, but then people who want to use a type you’ve defined deep in the hierarchy might have trouble finding out that type exists. They might also be annoyed at having to enter&#96;use my_crate::some_module::another_module::UsefulType;&#96; rather than&#96;use my_crate::UsefulType;&#96;.\n\nThe good news is that if the structure isn’t convenient for others to use from another library, you don’t have to rearrange your internal organization: Instead, you can re-export items to make a public structure that’s different from your private structure by using&#96;pub use&#96;. Re-exporting takes a public item in one location and makes it public in another location, as if it were defined in the other location instead.\n\nFor example, say we made a library named&#96;art&#96; for modeling artistic concepts. Within this library are two modules: a&#96;kinds&#96; module containing two enums named&#96;PrimaryColor&#96; and&#96;SecondaryColor&#96; and a&#96;utils&#96; module containing a function named&#96;mix&#96;, as shown in Listing 14-3.\n\nFilename: src/lib.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n//! # Art\n//!\n//! A library for modeling artistic concepts.\n\npub mod kinds {\n    /// The primary colors according to the RYB color model.\n    pub enum PrimaryColor {\n        Red,\n        Yellow,\n        Blue,\n    }\n\n    /// The secondary colors according to the RYB color model.\n    pub enum SecondaryColor {\n        Orange,\n        Green,\n        Purple,\n    }\n}\n\npub mod utils {\n    use crate::kinds::*;\n\n    /// Combines two primary colors in equal amounts to create\n    /// a secondary color.\n    pub fn mix(c1: PrimaryColor, c2: PrimaryColor) -\u003e SecondaryColor {\n        // --snip--\n        unimplemented!();\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-3: An&#96;art&#96; library with items organized into&#96;kinds&#96; and&#96;utils&#96; modules\n\nFigure 14-3 shows what the front page of the documentation for this crate generated by&#96;cargo doc&#96; would look like.\n\nFigure 14-3: The front page of the documentation for&#96;art&#96; that lists the&#96;kinds&#96; and&#96;utils&#96; modules\n\nNote that the&#96;PrimaryColor&#96; and&#96;SecondaryColor&#96; types aren’t listed on the front page, nor is the&#96;mix&#96; function. We have to click&#96;kinds&#96; and&#96;utils&#96; to see them.\n\nAnother crate that depends on this library would need&#96;use&#96; statements that bring the items from&#96;art&#96; into scope, specifying the module structure that’s currently defined. Listing 14-4 shows an example of a crate that uses the&#96;PrimaryColor&#96; and&#96;mix&#96; items from the&#96;art&#96; crate.\n\nFilename: src/main.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nuse art::kinds::PrimaryColor;\nuse art::utils::mix;\n\nfn main() {\n    let red = PrimaryColor::Red;\n    let yellow = PrimaryColor::Yellow;\n    mix(red, yellow);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-4: A crate using the&#96;art&#96; crate’s items with its internal structure exported\n\nThe author of the code in Listing 14-4, which uses the&#96;art&#96; crate, had to figure out that&#96;PrimaryColor&#96; is in the&#96;kinds&#96; module and&#96;mix&#96; is in the&#96;utils&#96; module. The module structure of the&#96;art&#96; crate is more relevant to developers working on the&#96;art&#96; crate than to those using it. The internal structure doesn’t contain any useful information for someone trying to understand how to use the&#96;art&#96; crate, but rather causes confusion because developers who use it have to figure out where to look, and must specify the module names in the&#96;use&#96; statements.\n\nTo remove the internal organization from the public API, we can modify the&#96;art&#96; crate code in Listing 14-3 to add&#96;pub use&#96; statements to re-export the items at the top level, as shown in Listing 14-5.\n\nFilename: src/lib.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n//! # Art\n//!\n//! A library for modeling artistic concepts.\n\npub use self::kinds::PrimaryColor;\npub use self::kinds::SecondaryColor;\npub use self::utils::mix;\n\npub mod kinds {\n    // --snip--\n    /// The primary colors according to the RYB color model.\n    pub enum PrimaryColor {\n        Red,\n        Yellow,\n        Blue,\n    }\n\n    /// The secondary colors according to the RYB color model.\n    pub enum SecondaryColor {\n        Orange,\n        Green,\n        Purple,\n    }\n}\n\npub mod utils {\n    // --snip--\n    use crate::kinds::*;\n\n    /// Combines two primary colors in equal amounts to create\n    /// a secondary color.\n    pub fn mix(c1: PrimaryColor, c2: PrimaryColor) -\u003e SecondaryColor {\n        SecondaryColor::Orange\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-5: Adding&#96;pub use&#96; statements to re-export items\n\nThe API documentation that&#96;cargo doc&#96; generates for this crate will now list and link re-exports on the front page, as shown in Figure 14-4, making the&#96;PrimaryColor&#96; and&#96;SecondaryColor&#96; types and the&#96;mix&#96; function easier to find.\n\nFigure 14-4: The front page of the documentation for&#96;art&#96; that lists the re-exports\n\nThe&#96;art&#96; crate users can still see and use the internal structure from Listing 14-3 as demonstrated in Listing 14-4, or they can use the more convenient structure in Listing 14-5, as shown in Listing 14-6.\n\nFilename: src/main.rs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nuse art::PrimaryColor;\nuse art::mix;\n\nfn main() {\n    // --snip--\n    let red = PrimaryColor::Red;\n    let yellow = PrimaryColor::Yellow;\n    mix(red, yellow);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nListing 14-6: A program using the re-exported items from the&#96;art&#96; crate\n\nIn cases where there are many nested modules, re-exporting the types at the top level with&#96;pub use&#96; can make a significant difference in the experience of people who use the crate. Another common use of&#96;pub use&#96; is to re-export definitions of a dependency in the current crate to make that crate’s definitions part of your crate’s public API.\n\nCreating a useful public API structure is more an art than a science, and you can iterate to find the API that works best for your users. Choosing&#96;pub use&#96; gives you flexibility in how you structure your crate internally and decouples that internal structure from what you present to your users. Look at some of the code of crates you’ve installed to see if their internal structure differs from their public API.\n\n### Setting Up a Crates.io Account\n\nBefore you can publish any crates, you need to create an account on [crates.io](https://crates.io/) and get an API token. To do so, visit the home page at [crates.io](https://crates.io/) and log in via a GitHub account. (The GitHub account is currently a requirement, but the site might support other ways of creating an account in the future.) Once you’re logged in, visit your account settings at [https://crates.io/me/](https://crates.io/me/) and retrieve your API key. Then, run the&#96;cargo login&#96; command and paste your API key when prompted, like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n$ cargo login\nabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz012345\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis command will inform Cargo of your API token and store it locally in ~/.cargo/credentials.toml. Note that this token is a secret: Do not share it with anyone else. If you do share it with anyone for any reason, you should revoke it and generate a new token on [crates.io](https://crates.io/).\n\n### Adding Metadata to a New Crate\n\nLet’s say you have a crate you want to publish. Before publishing, you’ll need to add some metadata in the&#96;[package]&#96; section of the crate’s Cargo.toml file.\n\nYour crate will need a unique name. While you’re working on a crate locally, you can name a crate whatever you’d like. However, crate names on [crates.io](https://crates.io/) are allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Once a crate name is taken, no one else can publish a crate with that name. Before attempting to publish a crate, search for the name you want to use. If the name has been used, you will need to find another name and edit the&#96;name&#96; field in the Cargo.toml file under the&#96;[package]&#96; section to use the new name for publishing, like so:\n\nFilename: Cargo.toml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[package]\nname = \"guessing_game\"\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEven if you’ve chosen a unique name, when you run&#96;cargo publish&#96; to publish the crate at this point, you’ll get a warning and then an error:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n$ cargo publish\n    Updating crates.io index\nwarning: manifest has no description, license, license-file, documentation, homepage or repository.\nSee https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html#package-metadata for more info.\n--snip--\nerror: failed to publish to registry at https://crates.io\n\nCaused by:\n  the remote server responded with an error (status 400 Bad Request): missing or empty metadata fields: description, license. Please see https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html for more information on configuring these fields\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis results in an error because you’re missing some crucial information: A description and license are required so that people will know what your crate does and under what terms they can use it. In Cargo.toml, add a description that’s just a sentence or two, because it will appear with your crate in search results. For the&#96;license&#96; field, you need to give a license identifier value. The [Linux Foundation’s Software Package Data Exchange (SPDX)](https://spdx.org/licenses/) lists the identifiers you can use for this value. For example, to specify that you’ve licensed your crate using the MIT License, add the&#96;MIT&#96; identifier:\n\nFilename: Cargo.toml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[package]\nname = \"guessing_game\"\nlicense = \"MIT\"\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you want to use a license that doesn’t appear in the SPDX, you need to place the text of that license in a file, include the file in your project, and then use&#96;license-file&#96; to specify the name of that file instead of using the&#96;license&#96; key.\n\nGuidance on which license is appropriate for your project is beyond the scope of this book. Many people in the Rust community license their projects in the same way as Rust by using a dual license of&#96;MIT OR Apache-2.0&#96;. This practice demonstrates that you can also specify multiple license identifiers separated by&#96;OR&#96; to have multiple licenses for your project.\n\nWith a unique name, the version, your description, and a license added, the Cargo.toml file for a project that is ready to publish might look like this:\n\nFilename: Cargo.toml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[package]\nname = \"guessing_game\"\nversion = \"0.1.0\"\nedition = \"2024\"\ndescription = \"A fun game where you guess what number the computer has chosen.\"\nlicense = \"MIT OR Apache-2.0\"\n\n[dependencies]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n[Cargo’s documentation](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/) describes other metadata you can specify to ensure that others can discover and use your crate more easily.\n\n### Publishing to Crates.io\n\nNow that you’ve created an account, saved your API token, chosen a name for your crate, and specified the required metadata, you’re ready to publish! Publishing a crate uploads a specific version to [crates.io](https://crates.io/) for others to use.\n\nBe careful, because a publish is permanent. The version can never be overwritten, and the code cannot be deleted except in certain circumstances. One major goal of Crates.io is to act as a permanent archive of code so that builds of all projects that depend on crates from [crates.io](https://crates.io/) will continue to work. Allowing version deletions would make fulfilling that goal impossible. However, there is no limit to the number of crate versions you can publish.\n\nRun the&#96;cargo publish&#96; command again. It should succeed now:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n$ cargo publish\n    Updating crates.io index\n   Packaging guessing_game v0.1.0 (file:///projects/guessing_game)\n    Packaged 6 files, 1.2KiB (895.0B compressed)\n   Verifying guessing_game v0.1.0 (file:///projects/guessing_game)\n   Compiling guessing_game v0.1.0\n(file:///projects/guessing_game/target/package/guessing_game-0.1.0)\n    Finished &#96;dev&#96; profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 0.19s\n   Uploading guessing_game v0.1.0 (file:///projects/guessing_game)\n    Uploaded guessing_game v0.1.0 to registry &#96;crates-io&#96;\nnote: waiting for &#96;guessing_game v0.1.0&#96; to be available at registry\n&#96;crates-io&#96;.\nYou may press ctrl-c to skip waiting; the crate should be available shortly.\n   Published guessing_game v0.1.0 at registry &#96;crates-io&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nCongratulations! You’ve now shared your code with the Rust community, and anyone can easily add your crate as a dependency of their project.\n\n### Publishing a New Version of an Existing Crate\n\nWhen you’ve made changes to your crate and are ready to release a new version, you change the&#96;version&#96; value specified in your Cargo.toml file and republish. Use the [Semantic Versioning rules](https://semver.org/) to decide what an appropriate next version number is, based on the kinds of changes you’ve made. Then, run&#96;cargo publish&#96; to upload the new version.\n\n### Deprecating Versions from Crates.io\n\nAlthough you can’t remove previous versions of a crate, you can prevent any future projects from adding them as a new dependency. This is useful when a crate version is broken for one reason or another. In such situations, Cargo supports yanking a crate version.\n\nYanking a version prevents new projects from depending on that version while allowing all existing projects that depend on it to continue. Essentially, a yank means that all projects with a Cargo.lock will not break, and any future Cargo.lock files generated will not use the yanked version.\n\nTo yank a version of a crate, in the directory of the crate that you’ve previously published, run&#96;cargo yank&#96; and specify which version you want to yank. For example, if we’ve published a crate named&#96;guessing_game&#96; version 1.0.1 and we want to yank it, then we’d run the following in the project directory for&#96;guessing_game&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n$ cargo yank --vers 1.0.1\n    Updating crates.io index\n        Yank guessing_game@1.0.1\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nBy adding&#96;--undo&#96; to the command, you can also undo a yank and allow projects to start depending on a version again:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n$ cargo yank --vers 1.0.1 --undo\n    Updating crates.io index\n      Unyank guessing_game@1.0.1\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nA yank does not delete any code. It cannot, for example, delete accidentally uploaded secrets. If that happens, you must reset those secrets immediately.",
  "url": "https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch14-02-publishing-to-crates-io.html"
}
```

{
  "markdown": "Motivation - Cargo Vet\n\n- Light (default)\n- Rust\n- Coal\n- Navy\n- Ayu\n\n# Cargo Vet\n\n# Motivation\n\nThe discussion below covers the high-level motivation for building this system. If you're just interested in how it works, you can skip to the next section.\n\n### Security Risks of Third-Party Code\n\nLow-friction reuse of third-party components — via systems like crates.io or npm — is an essential element of modern software development. Unfortunately, it also widens the set of actors who can introduce a security vulnerability into the final product.\n\nThese defects can be honest mistakes, or intentional supply-chain attacks. They can exist in the initial version, or be introduced later as an update. They can be introduced by the original author, or by a new maintainer who acquires control over the release of subsequent versions. Taken together, these avenues constitute a demonstrated and growing risk to software security.\n\nIdeally, the composition model would include technical guarantees to isolate components from each other and prevent a defect in one component from compromising the security of the entire program (e.g. [WebAssembly nanoprocesses](https://bytecodealliance.org/articles/announcing-the-bytecode-alliance)). However, that is often not a realistic solution for many projects today. In the absence of technical guarantees, the responsibility for ensuring software integrity falls to humans. But reviewing every line of third-party code can be very time-consuming and difficult, and undermines the original premise of low-friction code reuse. Practically speaking, it often just doesn't happen — even at large well-resourced companies.\n\n### Tackling This in Rust\n\nThere are two properties of Rust that make this problem easier to solve.\n\nFirst, it's relatively easy to audit Rust code. Unlike C/C++, Rust code is memory-safe by default, and unlike JavaScript, there is no highly-dynamic shared global environment. This means that you can often reason at a high level about the range of a module's potential behavior without carefully studying all of its internal invariants. For example, a complicated string parser with a narrow interface, no unsafe code, and no powerful imports has limited means to compromise the rest of the program. This also makes it easier to conclude that a new version is safe based on a diff from a prior trusted version.\n\nSecond, nearly everyone in the Rust ecosystem relies on the same set of basic tooling — Cargo and crates.io — to import and manage third-party components, and there is high overlap in the dependency sets. For example, at the time of writing, [Firefox](https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/file/add572d6012047244d022436e0b5c578b3dd7cf7/Cargo.lock), [wasmtime](https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wasmtime/blob/49c2b1e60a87623796046176500bed6afa956d2f/Cargo.lock), and [the Rust compiler](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/blob/532d3cda90b8a729cd982548649d32803d265052/Cargo.lock) specified 406, 310, and 357 crates.io dependencies respectively1. Ignoring version, each project shares about half of its dependencies with at least one of the other two projects, and 107 dependencies are common across all three.\n\nThis creates opportunities to share the analysis burden in an systematic way. If you're able to discover that a trusted party has already audited the exact crate release you're using, you can gain quite a bit of confidence in its integrity with no additional effort. If that party has audited a different version, you could consider either switching to it, or merely auditing the diff between the two. Not every organization and project share the same level of risk tolerance, but there is a lot of common ground, and substantial room for improvement beyond no sharing at all.\n\n## Footnotes\n\n1\n\nThe following command string computes the names of the crates.io packages specified in&#96;Cargo.lock&#96;. Note the filtering for path and git dependencies, along with removing duplicates due to different versions of the same crate:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ngrep -e \"name = \" -e \"source = \\\"registry\" Cargo.lock | awk '/source =/ { print prv_line; next } { prv_line = $0 }' | sort -u\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",
  "url": "https://mozilla.github.io/cargo-vet/motivation.html"
}
```

```json
{
  "markdown": "# Motivation\n\nWhen you first started building your game, you may have modelled every content option as an enum. Character classes, buildings, towns, platformer levels, loot...\nThis is great for prototyping: enums are simple to set up, and hard to screw up because of exhaustive pattern matching.\n\nHowever, enums require exhaustive enumeration. Adding another item type requires adding an arm to *everywhere* that this is being used, and you can't just add more items off in their own module without updating every single call site.\n\nSo instead, we could use traits! Traits are extensible: great for this sort of \"open set with shared properties\" abstraction.\nWhile traits can be a nuisance (you almost certainly need to ensure your trait is object-safe!), they can be a nice fit for a lot of games or forms of content.\n\nBut then you realize either:\n\n1. You want your game to be easily moddable, by people who don't have access to your source code and don't know Rust.\n2. You have artists and game designers on your team who want to be able to just quickly author new content without having to change the code at all.\n\nThere's a standard solution to this: **data-driven** content.\nRather than defining your variants in code, define their properties using data that you can store on a hard drive,\nand then use the variations in the supplied fields to create a rich variation in gameplay by parsing these properties inside your game.\n\n## Basics of implementing data-driven content\n\nIn a data-driven design architecture, data must be transformed from the **serialized format**, which is stored on the disk, into the **in-game representation**, which is used by game systems and logic to actually interact in the game world.\n\nWe can start with a very simple architecture:\n\n1. Store a string corresponding to each type of game object that matches the file name of the serialized data.\n2. When we want to spawn an object of that type, pass in that string.\n3. During the spawning process, deserialize that file directly into a game object.\n4. Spawn the generated game object.\n\n## More sophisticated data-driven content\n\nNow, there are several areas for improvement.\nLet's go over them now, and then tackle them one at a time:\n\n1. Strings are heavy identifiers: as long as we can convert to and from a nice human-readable string, we don't care about actually storing that identifier everywhere.\n2. Constantly deserializing from disk is very slow.\n3. Similarly, some final game objects are very heavy: we want to look up the data when we need it, rather than storing thousands of copies of it for every object.\n4. We can't always translate directly from the serialized to in-game representation. This is particularly common and important when we need to reference *other* serialized content inside of our game object. It also comes up when you want to simplify the serialized representation to make it more space efficient or easier to author.\n\nThere are a few additional problems, which Leafwing has chosen to leave as \"future work\" for now:\n\n1. Text-based file formats are easy to mess up: tools for validating these would be very useful.\n2. Text-based file formats are easy to mess up: it would be great to have designer-friendly tools for authoring them.\n\n## Efficient identifiers\n\nStrings are a very natural fit for identifiers in the context of game development:\nevery asset needs its own file name anyways, and we need to be able to talk about our different bits of content.\n\nHowever, strings are relatively slow and cumbersome and initial experiments quickly ran into performance problems.\nBut what makes a good identifier?\nIdentifiers should be:\n\n1. **Compact:** taking up space hurts both memory usage and the speed of all operations.\n2. **Stable:** adding another game object shouldn't break all of your existing IDs.\n3. **Low-collision:** game objects should have a low risk of colliding with each other.\n4. **Const-constructable:** defining constants representing a game object reduces the risk of typos and eases refactoring.\n5. **Type-safe:** it should be impossible to accidently use the id for a monster where the id for a piece of loot is called for.\n\nTo achieve the first four properties, we hash the names of our game objects, using the stored hashes as our opaque identifiers.\nFinally, we achieve some degree of type-safety by adding a marker generic to each &#96;Id&#96;, which corresponds to the type of manifest that it indexes.\n\n## Object storage deduplication\n\nWe can use the generated manifest to meet two of our other goals: avoiding constant deserialization and deduplicating heavy data.\n\nThe manifest (or a central asset server) holds a single, canonical copy of each game object.\n\nDepending on your needs, you can either look up the value as it is needed (using the unique key), or copy it directly onto your game object, generally as components.\nThe latter is generally going to result in faster iteration (and random access via &#96;query.get&#96;), but will lead to increased memory costs.\n\n## Intermediate deserialization formats\n\nJumping directly from the serialized form to the in-game form of an asset is often not particularly feasible or fun. For example:\n\n1. Data-driven game objects commonly want to reference *other* data-driven game object.\nThis comes up when dealing with things like \"loot drop tables\", \"enemy spawn rates for each level\", or \"allowable terrain for buildings\".\n2. You may need to reference other assets, such as meshes or sound effects, that require a &#96;Handle&#96; from Bevy's &#96;AssetServer&#96;.\n3. Parsing and storing enum-like values from string-based formats like JSON can be fraught with footguns.\n\nRather than encouraging users to build progressively more complex (and fragile) serializers and deserializers, &#96;leafwing_manifest&#96; takes a pragmatic approach:\n\n1. For each manifest, users may create a permissive, intermediate **raw manifest** data structure that maps directly to the constraints of the underlying file format (e.g. CSV or JSON). If you need a manual &#96;Deserialize&#96; impl it's too complicated.\n2. Load all of the raw manifests first.\n3. Once all raw manifests are loaded, attempt to convert them into their final **processed manifest** forms, cross-referencing other manifests as needed.\n\nThis conversion process is flexible and direct, and involves ordinary synchronous Rust code. Here's the heart of the &#96;Manifest&#96; trait:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nuse bevy::prelude::*;\nuse std::error::Error;\n\ntrait Manifest: Sized + Resource {\n    /// The raw data type that is loaded from disk.\n    type RawManifest: Asset + for\u003c'de\u003e Deserialize\u003c'de\u003e;\n\n    /// The raw data type that is stored in the manifest.\n    type RawItem;\n\n    /// The type of the game object stored in the manifest.\n    type Item;\n\n    /// The error type that can occur when converting raw manifests into a manifest.\n    type ConversionError: Error;\n\n    /// The format of the raw manifest on disk.\n    const FORMAT: ManifestFormat;\n\n    fn from_raw_manifest(\n        raw_manifest: Self::RawManifest,\n        _world: \u0026mut World,\n    ) -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, Self::Err\u003e;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you don't require this intermediate step, you can simply leave it out! Set &#96;Err = Infallible&#96;, &#96;RawManifest=Self&#96; and &#96;RawItem=Item&#96; to avoid added work or complexity.\n\nIf you've ever written a &#96;TryFrom&#96; impl in Rust before, this should be very familiar to you.\nAttempt to convert the items in the collection, one at a time, returning errors via the &#96;?&#96; operator as needed.\nThe only wrinkle is that we *also* have access to the entire Bevy &#96;World&#96;, allowing us to access asset data or other manifests as needed.\n",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Leafwing-Studios/leafwing_manifest/main/MOTIVATION.md"
}
```
User decision: **accepted**

Status: **completed**

```json
{
  "markdown": "# Motivation for H26Forge\n\nTo see why a tool like H26Forge is valuable, we'll go through an example of modifying syntax elements manually. You can follow along with the [H.264 spec](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-H.264-202108-I/en).\n\n## Observation\n\nSuppose that we find a decoder that is missing a bounds check for &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96;, which is used as a loop bound for parsing &#96;offset_for_ref_frame[]&#96; values.\n\n![pic_order_cnt_type SPS snippet with num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle highlighted.](img/sps_pic_order_cnt_type_highlight.png)\n\nWe would like to create a proof-of-concept video that demonstrates what can go wrong with this missing bounds check.\nOur proof-of-concept encoded video needs to do three things:\n1. Reach this parsing code.\n2. Have an out-of-bounds &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96;.\n3. Have enough &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; values.\n\nWe will first show why satisfying these three objects manually is possible, but challenging, and how H26Forge simplifies this process.\n\n## Manually Modifying Syntax Elements\n\nWe will modify &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; to be 1, &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96; to be 300, and each &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; value to 0. Each of these syntax elements is entropy encoded with the &#96;ue(v)&#96; or &#96;se(v)&#96; functions. These are unsigned and signed [exp-Golomb](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exponential-Golomb_coding) codings, respectively, which are variable length codes. The benefit of using exp-Golomb is that smaller, more likely values take up less space.\n\nFor a manual modification of syntax elements, we could try to find an example H.264 video that may get us most of the way there, but since the syntax elements are small enough we will work with [input_vids/SPS_PPS_I_P.264](../input_vids/SPS_PPS_I_P.264) inside a hex editor. To view the syntax elements, you can use [FFmpeg](https://developer.ridgerun.com/wiki/index.php/H264_Analysis_Tools#Display_Nal_Unit_.2F_Slice_Information), the [H.264 reference decoder](https://iphome.hhi.de/suehring/tml/download/), or any [H.264](https://github.com/aizvorski/h264bitstream) [bitstream](https://github.com/IENT/YUView) [analyzer](https://mradionov.github.io/h264-bitstream-viewer/). Keep in mind that because we are setting the syntax elements to be out-of-bounds values, these tools may not decode the stream correctly.\n\n### 1. Reaching this parsing Code\n\nTo reach &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96;, we need to set &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; to 1. Looking at the output of the bitstream analyzer for &#96;SPS_PPS_I_P.264&#96;, we see that &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; is currently set to 0:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nAnnex B NALU w/ long startcode, len 10, forbidden_bit 0, nal_reference_idc 3, nal_unit_type 7\n\n\tSPS: profile_idc                                                (100)\n\tSPS: constraint_set0_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set1_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set2_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set3_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set4_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set5_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: reserved_zero_2bits                                        (0)\n\tSPS: level_idc                                                  (11)\n\tSPS: seq_parameter_set_id                                       (0)\n\tSPS: chroma_format_idc                                          (1)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_luma_minus8                                      (0)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_chroma_minus8                                    (0)\n\tSPS: qpprime_y_zero_transform_bypass_flag                       (false)\n\tSPS: seq_scaling_matrix_present_flag                            (false)\n\tSPS: log2_max_frame_num_minus4                                  (0)\n\tSPS: pic_order_cnt_type                                         (0)      \u003c-- Need to change this to 1\n\tSPS: log2_max_pic_order_cnt_lsb_minus4                          (2)\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nThe exp-Golomb encoding of 0 is the single bit &#96;b1&#96;, and the encoding of 1 is the bit sequence &#96;b010&#96;. This means that we have to insert two more bits into the file. We need to first identify where in the file &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; is encoded:\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_hex.png)\n\nBy either manually encoding the preceding SPS syntax elements, or through trial-and-error, we identify the 2nd big-endian bit in &#96;0xD&#96; located at 0x9 corresponds to the 1 encoding.\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264 with encoded pic_order_cnt_type annotated.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_hex_pic_order_cnt_type_encoding.png)\n\nSo to set this to the recovered value of 1, we change the binary from &#96;b1[1]01&#96; to &#96;b1[010]&#96; or from &#96;0xD&#96; to &#96;0xA&#96;. At the moment, we are overwriting the hex and ignoring subsequently decoded syntax elements.\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264 with pic_order_cnt_type changed.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_change1.png)\n\nRunning this new file through our bitstream analyzer we see that the &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; value has been updated, but the rest of the bitstream leads to unexpected values.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nAnnex B NALU w/ long startcode, len 10, forbidden_bit 0, nal_reference_idc 3, nal_unit_type 7\n\n\tSPS: profile_idc                                                (100)\n\tSPS: constraint_set0_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set1_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set2_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set3_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set4_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set5_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: reserved_zero_2bits                                        (0)\n\tSPS: level_idc                                                  (11)\n\tSPS: seq_parameter_set_id                                       (0)\n\tSPS: chroma_format_idc                                          (1)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_luma_minus8                                      (0)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_chroma_minus8                                    (0)\n\tSPS: qpprime_y_zero_transform_bypass_flag                       (false)\n\tSPS: seq_scaling_matrix_present_flag                            (false)\n\tSPS: log2_max_frame_num_minus4                                  (0)\n\tSPS: pic_order_cnt_type                                         (1)         // Has been updated!\n\tSPS: delta_pic_order_always_zero_flag                           (true)      // The rest of the bitstream is now off\n\tSPS: offset_for_non_ref_pic                                     (-2)\n\tSPS: offset_for_top_to_bottom_field                             (9)\n\tSPS: num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle                      (2)         // Target syntax element\n\tSPS: offset_for_ref_frame                                       (-1)\n\tSPS: offset_for_ref_frame                                       (0)\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nBecause we don't care about the values up to our target, we can continue onward.\n\n### 2. Set &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96; to an out-of-bounds value\n\nTo set &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96; to 300, we need to change its existing exp-Golomb encoding of 2 (&#96;b011&#96;) to 300 (&#96;b00000000100101101&#96;). For reference, [here](http://wobblycucumber.blogspot.com/2013/05/unsigned-exponential-golomb-codes.html) is an online exp-Golomb encoder/decoder.\n\nAgain, through manually encoding the bitstream values, or through trial-and-error, we can find the location of the currently encoded &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96; to be inside of &#96;0x4D&#96; at 0xB:\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264 with num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle highlighted.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_hex_num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle_encoding.png)\n\nLooking at the bit-sequence, the value 2 is encoded inside &#96;0x4D&#96; at &#96;b010[0 11]01&#96;. We will rewrite this by inserting &#96;b00000000100101101&#96; or &#96;b0 0000 0001 0010 1101&#96; or &#96;0x012D&#96; after the &#96;0x4&#96;:\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264 with num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle changed.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_change2.png)\n\nLooking at the bitstream again, we see that we were successful in our modification but we get a crash that prevents us from reading more &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; values:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nAnnex B NALU w/ long startcode, len 10, forbidden_bit 0, nal_reference_idc 3, nal_unit_type 7\n\n\tSPS: profile_idc                                                (100)\n\tSPS: constraint_set0_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set1_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set2_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set3_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set4_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set5_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: reserved_zero_2bits                                        (0)\n\tSPS: level_idc                                                  (11)\n\tSPS: seq_parameter_set_id                                       (0)\n\tSPS: chroma_format_idc                                          (1)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_luma_minus8                                      (0)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_chroma_minus8                                    (0)\n\tSPS: qpprime_y_zero_transform_bypass_flag                       (false)\n\tSPS: seq_scaling_matrix_present_flag                            (false)\n\tSPS: log2_max_frame_num_minus4                                  (0)\n\tSPS: pic_order_cnt_type                                         (1)\n\tSPS: delta_pic_order_always_zero_flag                           (true)\n\tSPS: offset_for_non_ref_pic                                     (-2)\n\tSPS: offset_for_top_to_bottom_field                             (9)\n\tSPS: num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle                      (300)       \u003c-- Set to 300\n\tSPS: offset_for_ref_frame                                       (1)         // Run out of bits to continue\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNow we will insert more bytes into the bitstream to have enough &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; values.\n\n### 3. Include enough &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; to overflow\n\nOur modifications have led us to the end of the SPS, so we need to insert more bytes into the bitstream to ensure we will have enough &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; values to decode.\n\nFor simplicity, we will set each &#96;offset_for_ref_frame&#96; value to 0 which is an exp-Golomb encoding of &#96;b1&#96;. This means we insert 75 &#96;0xF&#96;s into the bitstream, and some more for the rest of the bitstream:\n\n![Hex dump of SPS_SPS_I_P.264 with offset_for_ref_frame encodings inserted.](img/sps_pps_i_p.264_change3.png)\n\nAnd now we get the following syntax elements:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nAnnex B NALU w/ long startcode, len 65, forbidden_bit 0, nal_reference_idc 3, nal_unit_type 7\n\n\tSPS: profile_idc                                                (100)\n\tSPS: constraint_set0_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set1_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set2_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set3_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set4_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: constraint_set5_flag                                       (false)\n\tSPS: reserved_zero_2bits                                        (0)\n\tSPS: level_idc                                                  (11)\n\tSPS: seq_parameter_set_id                                       (0)\n\tSPS: chroma_format_idc                                          (1)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_luma_minus8                                      (0)\n\tSPS: bit_depth_chroma_minus8                                    (0)\n\tSPS: qpprime_y_zero_transform_bypass_flag                       (false)\n\tSPS: seq_scaling_matrix_present_flag                            (false)\n\tSPS: log2_max_frame_num_minus4                                  (0)\n\tSPS: pic_order_cnt_type                                         (1)\n\tSPS: delta_pic_order_always_zero_flag                           (true)\n\tSPS: offset_for_non_ref_pic                                     (-2)\n\tSPS: offset_for_top_to_bottom_field                             (9)\n\tSPS: num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle                      (300)\n\tSPS: offset_for_ref_frame                                       (0)\n... A total of 298 more all 0\n\tSPS: offset_for_ref_frame                                       (0)\n...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Summary\n\nThrough this manual process, we now have a PoC to write all 0s after a missing bounds-check for &#96;num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle&#96;. It would require more work in order to fix the rest of the bitstream to make the decoder continue decoding after this.\n\nNow let's see how this process is simplified with H26Forge.\n\n## Modifying with H26Forge\n\nWith H26Forge, we can follow the syntax elements in the H.264 spec, and completely ignore any kind of bitstream encoding or hex editing. All the above changes get summarized to the following Python code:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# ./h26forge modify -i input_vids/SPS_PPS_I_P.264 -o oob_num_ref_frames.264 -t transforms/oob_num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle.py\ndef oob_num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle(ds):\n    value_to_write = 0\n    ds[\"spses\"][0][\"pic_order_cnt_type\"] = 1\n    ds[\"spses\"][0][\"num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle\"] = 300\n    ds[\"spses\"][0][\"offset_for_ref_frame\"] = [value_to_write] * ds[\"spses\"][0][\"num_ref_frames_in_pic_order_cnt_cycle\"]\n\n    # New addition\n    for i in range(len(ds[\"slices\"])):\n        ds[\"slices\"][i][\"sh\"][\"delta_pic_order_cnt\"] = [0, 0]\n    return ds\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWe can see in the snippet that we can directly set the syntax elements to whatever value we would like. This is because H26Forge handles the entropy decoding/encoding so all you have to do is focus on syntax element manipulation. One addition to note is the for loop at the bottom. This is because &#96;pic_order_cnt_type&#96; is used in slice header decoding, so the decoder expects those values if it continues to decode the bitstream:\n\n![delta_pic_order_cnt depends on pic_order_cnt_type in slice header decoding.](img/slice_header_delta_pic_order_cnt.png)\n\n## Conclusion\n\nH26Forge greatly simplifies syntax element manipulation of H.264 encoded videos allowing for the creation of syntactically-correct but semantically non-compliant videos.",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/h26forge/h26forge/main/docs/MOTIVATION.md"
}

java.util.HashMap;\\\\\\\\nimport java.util.HashSet;\\\\\\\\nimport java.util.List;\\\\\\\\nimport java.util.Map;\\\\\\\\nimport java.util.Set;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * 部件分类约束执行器实现类\\\\\\\\n * 专门处理部件分类相关的约束求解\\\\\\\\n *\\\\\\\\n * @since 2025-01-XX\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\n@Slf4j\\\\\\\\npublic class PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl extends ModuleBaseConstraintExecutorImpl {\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    public PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl() {\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 处理部件分类约束\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param partCategoryReq 部件分类请求\\\\\\\\n     * @return 推理结果\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    @Override\\\\\\\\n    public Result\u003cList\u003cModuleInst\u003e\u003e doProcess(InferPartCategoryReq partCategoryReq) {\\\\\\\\n        try {\\\\\\\\n            // 查找原始部件分类\\\\\\\\n            PartCategory originalCategory = module.getPartCategory(partCategoryReq.getPartCategoryCode());\\\\\\\\n            if (originalCategory == null) {\\\\\\\\n                log.error(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PartCategory not found: {}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", partCategoryReq.getPartCategoryCode());\\\\\\\\n                return Result.failed(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PartCategory not found: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" + partCategoryReq.getPartCategoryCode());\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            log.info(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Priority-orginal aparts: {}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", originalCategory.getAllAtomicPartShortString());\\\\\\\\n            // 遍历所有部件约束请求，逐步过滤\\\\\\\\n            PartCategory filteredCategory = originalCategory;\\\\\\\\n            List\u003cPartCategoryInputBase\u003e partCategoryInputs = new ArrayList\u003c\u003e();\\\\\\\\n            for (PartConstraintReq partConstraintReq : partCategoryReq.getPartConstraintReqs()) {\\\\\\\\n                // 一条partConstraintReq会被拆分两条规则，一条是过滤规则（根据where条件过滤），一条是约束规则（根据attrCode,\\\\\\\\n                // attrComparator, attrValue构建约束表达式）\\\\\\\\n                // 查询满足条件的PartCategory\\\\\\\\n                filteredCategory = filteredCategory.filterClone(partConstraintReq);\\\\\\\\n                String msg = String.format(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Priority-filtered aparts: {%s} by {%s}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n                        filteredCategory.getAllAtomicPartShortString(),\\\\\\\\n                        partConstraintReq.toShortString());\\\\\\\\n                log.info(msg);\\\\\\\\n                if (filteredCategory.getAllAtomicPartShortString().isEmpty()) {\\\\\\\\n                    return Result.noSolution(msg);\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n                PartCategoryInput partCategoryInput = PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl.toPartCategoryInput(\\\\\\\\n                        filteredCategory, partConstraintReq);\\\\\\\\n                partCategoryInputs.add(partCategoryInput);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            ModuleInput moduleInput = new ModuleInput();\\\\\\\\n            moduleInput.setPartCategoryInputs(partCategoryInputs);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            SolverResult sr = null;\\\\\\\\n            // 检查是否有优先级规则，如果有则使用分级求解\\\\\\\\n            Module moduleClone = module.clone();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            if (filteredCategory.hasPriorityRule()) {\\\\\\\\n                PartCategory mergedFilteredCategory = buildMergedFilteredCategory(originalCategory,\\\\\\\\n                        partCategoryInputs);\\\\\\\\n                moduleClone.addPartCategory(mergedFilteredCategory);\\\\\\\\n                sr = solveWithPriorityConstraints(\\\\\\\\n                        moduleClone, moduleInput);\\\\\\\\n            } else {\\\\\\\\n                moduleClone.addPartCategory(filteredCategory);\\\\\\\\n                sr = solveWithOutPriorityConstraints(moduleClone, moduleInput);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            return Result.success(sr.getSolutions());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        } catch (Exception e) {\\\\\\\\n            log.error(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Failed to process part category constraint\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", e);\\\\\\\\n            return Result.failed(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Failed to process part category constraint: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" + e.getMessage());\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 将部件列表添加到合并的原子部件Map中\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param mergedAtomicParts 合并的原子部件Map\\\\\\\\n     * @param parts             部件列表\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    private void addPartsToMap(Map\u003cString, Part\u003e mergedAtomicParts, List\u003cPart\u003e parts) {\\\\\\\\n        for (Part part : parts) {\\\\\\\\n            mergedAtomicParts.put(part.getCode(), part);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 构建合并后的过滤分类\\\\\\\\n     * 仅支持两层结果，不构建结构\\\\\\\\n     * 使用扣除法原则：\\\\\\\\n     * - 含有父分类，则求并集\\\\\\\\n     * - 不含有父分类，补充没有在里面的子分类的部件\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param originalCategory   原始部件分类\\\\\\\\n     * @param partCategoryInputs 部件约束列表\\\\\\\\n     * @return 合并后的过滤分类\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    private PartCategory buildMergedFilteredCategory(PartCategory originalCategory,\\\\\\\\n            List\u003cPartCategoryInputBase\u003e partCategoryInputs) {\\\\\\\\n        // 实现案例\\\\\\\\n        // --drive PC\\\\\\\\n        // ----sd PC\\\\\\\\n        // -------sd1 part\\\\\\\\n        // -------sd2 part\\\\\\\\n        // -------sd3 part\\\\\\\\n        // ----md PC\\\\\\\\n        // -------md1 part\\\\\\\\n        // -------md2 part\\\\\\\\n        // -------md3 part\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // 含有父，则求并集\\\\\\\\n        // input1: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{drive-\u003e md1} --\u003e mReq={sd1,sd2,md1}\\\\\\\\n        // input1: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{drive-\u003e sd1} --\u003e mReq={sd1,sd2}\\\\\\\\n        // 不还原其层次结构\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // 不含有父，补充没有在里面的子分类的部件\\\\\\\\n        // input1: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{md-\u003emd1} --\u003e mReq={sd1,sd2,md1} // 各个孩子都有\\\\\\\\n        // *input2: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{sd-\u003esd1} --\u003e mReq={sd1,sd2,md1,md2,md3} 补充\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        Map\u003cString, Part\u003e mergedAtomicParts = new HashMap\u003c\u003e();\\\\\\\\n        Set\u003cString\u003e allPartCategoryCodes = new HashSet\u003c\u003e();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // 收集所有约束请求的分类代码和原子部件\\\\\\\\n        for (PartCategoryInputBase ipt : partCategoryInputs) {\\\\\\\\n            if (!(ipt instanceof PartCategoryInput)) {\\\\\\\\n                log.warn(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"ipt is not PartCategoryInput, code: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" + ipt.getPartCategoryCode());\\\\\\\\n                continue;\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            PartCategoryInput partConstraint = (PartCategoryInput) ipt;\\\\\\\\n            String categoryCode = partConstraint.getOrgReq().getPartCategoryCode();\\\\\\\\n            allPartCategoryCodes.add(categoryCode);\\\\\\\\n            // 合并原子部件\\\\\\\\n            List\u003cPart\u003e atomicParts = partConstraint.getFilteredCategory().getAllAtomicParts();\\\\\\\\n            addPartsToMap(mergedAtomicParts, atomicParts);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // 判断父分类是否包含在allPartCategoryCodes中\\\\\\\\n        if (!allPartCategoryCodes.contains(originalCategory.getCode())) {\\\\\\\\n            // 要把没有包含的子分类的部件补充上\\\\\\\\n            List\u003cPartCategory\u003e partCategoryList = originalCategory.getPartCategorys();\\\\\\\\n            for (PartCategory subPartCategory : partCategoryList) {\\\\\\\\n                if (!allPartCategoryCodes.contains(subPartCategory.getCode())) {\\\\\\\\n                    // 补充未包含的子分类的所有原子部件\\\\\\\\n                    List\u003cPart\u003e subAtomicParts = subPartCategory.getAllAtomicParts();\\\\\\\\n                    addPartsToMap(mergedAtomicParts, subAtomicParts);\\\\\\\\n                }\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        // 克隆原始分类并添加合并的部件\\\\\\\\n        PartCategory resultCategory = originalCategory.clone();\\\\\\\\n        resultCategory.addAtomicPartsWithoutStructure(new ArrayList\u003c\u003e(mergedAtomicParts.values()));\\\\\\\\n        return resultCategory;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":174}\\\",\\\"toolCall\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"readToolCall\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"args\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\01.code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\mygithub\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\jmixconfigengine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\src\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\main\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\java\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\com\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\jmix\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\executor\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\impl\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\PartCategoryConstraintExecutorImpl.java\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"result\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"package 不含有父，补充没有在里面的子分类的部件\\\\\\\\n        // input1: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{md-\u003emd1} --\u003e mReq={sd1,sd2,md1} // 各个孩子都有\\\\\\\\n        // *input2: req1:{sd-\u003esd1,sd2} req2:{sd-\u003esd1} *\\\\\\\\n     * @param partCategoryInst 部件分类实例\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    public void addPartCategoryInst(PartCategoryInst partCategoryInst) {\\\\\\\\n        partCategorys.add(partCategoryInst);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 根据部件代码查询部件实例（从所有部件中查询）\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param partCode 部件代码\\\\\\\\n     * @return 部件实例，如果不存在则返回null\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    @JsonIgnore\\\\\\\\n    public PartInst queryPart(String partCategoryCode, int instId, String partCode) {\\\\\\\\n        // 先在当前层查找\\\\\\\\n        if (Strings.isNullOrEmpty(partCategoryCode)) {\\\\\\\\n            return super.queryPart(partCode);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        // 在当前层查找\\\\\\\\n        for (PartCategoryInst partCategoryInst : partCategorys) {\\\\\\\\n            if (partCategoryInst.getCode().equals(partCategoryCode) \u0026\u0026 partCategoryInst.getInstanceId() == instId) {\\\\\\\\n                return partCategoryInst.queryPart(partCode);\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        return null;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 添加优先级属性值信息到字符串构建器\\\\\\\\n     * (priorityAttrValues == null || priorityAttrValues.isEmpty()) {\\\\\\\\n            return;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        if (sb.length() \u003e 0) {\\\\\\\\n            sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PAs(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        boolean first = true;\\\\\\\\n        for (PriorityAttrValue priorityAttrValue : priorityAttrValues) {\\\\\\\\n            if (!first) {\\\\\\\\n                sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n sb.append(shortCode).append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").append(priorityAttrValue.getOptimalValue());\\\\\\\\n            first = false;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 获取优先级属性短编码字符串\\\\\\\\n     * 输出 attrCode 和 shortCode 的关系\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @return 优先级属性短编码字符串，格式：shortCode:attrCode，多个用逗号分隔\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    @JsonIgnore\\\\\\\\n    public String getPriorityAttrShortCodeStr() {\\\\\\\\n        if (priorityAttrValues == null || priorityAttrValues.isEmpty()) {\\\\\\\\n            return \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\";\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();\\\\\\\\n        sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PAs:priorityAttrValues \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PO:priorityOverallValue \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\n                .append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"PS:prioritySortNo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        boolean first = true;\\\\\\\\n        for (PriorityAttrValue priorityAttrValue : priorityAttrValues) {\\\\\\\\n            String attrCode = priorityAttrValue.getAttrCode();\\\\\\\\n            if (attrCode == null || attrCode.isEmpty()) {\\\\\\\\n                continue;\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            if (!first) {\\\\\\\\n                sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            }\\\\\\\\n            String shortCode = toAttrShortCode(attrCode);\\\\\\\\n            sb.append(shortCode).append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").append(attrCode);\\\\\\\\n            first = false;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        sb.append(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\")\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n        return sb.toString();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 将属性代码转换为短编码\\\\\\\\n     * 取 attrCode 的前两位，转换为大写\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param attrCode 属性代码\\\\\\\\n     * @return 短编码，如果 attrCode 为 null 或长度不足则返回空字符串\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    private String toAttrShortCode(String attrCode) {\\\\\\\\n        if 2).toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        return attrCode.toUpperCase();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 生成短字符串表示（包含优先级信息）\\\\\\\\n     *\\\\\\\\n     * @param isSimple appendPriorityAttrValuesInfo(sb);\\\\\\\\n        return sb.toString();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    private void toShortString(StringBuilder sb, boolean isSimple, index++;\\\\\\\\n            sb.append(partCategory.toShortString(isSimple, partCategory.getInstanceId()));\\\\\\\\n            if (!(index == this.partCategorys.size())) { // isLast\\\\\\\\n wledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-04-26T22:47:23.867Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"call_function_jxzs5tg8z615_1\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"call_function_jxzs5tg8z615_1\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\01.code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\mygithub\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\jmixconfigengine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\doc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\基于约束求解的反推参数的设计文档.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"limit\\\\\\\":150}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\01.code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\mygithub\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\jmixconfigengine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\doc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\基于约束求解的反推参数的设计文档.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"limit\\\\\\\":150,\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"d:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\01.code\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\mygithub\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\jmixconfigengine\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\doc\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\基于约束求解的反推参数的设计文档.md\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# 基于约束求解的反推参数的设计文档\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. 概述\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1.1 设计目标\\\\\\\\n设计一个基于约束求解的反推参数系统，能够根据已知的部件数量和参数值，通过约束求解技术反推出其他未知参数的值。该系统采用图调度机制实现差量加载，支持多种约束类型和复杂的业务规则。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1.2 核心功能\\\\\\\\n- 基于Ortools SAT约束求解器的参数反推\\\\\\\\n- 支持差量加载和全量加载两种模式\\\\\\\\n- 提供图调度机制优化求解性能\\\\\\\\n- 支持多种约束类型（兼容性、计算、选择等）\\\\\\\\n- 提供完整的南向扩展接口\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1.3 使用流程\\\\\\\\n1. 初始化约束执行器并添加模块\\\\\\\\n2. 构建约束模型和变量映射\\\\\\\\n3. 通过图调度机制确定需要加载的规则和变量\\\\\\\\n4. 执行约束求解获取参数反推结果\\\\\\\\n5. 后处理优化和结果验证\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. 架构设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2.1 整体架构\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n北向接口 (ModuleConstraintExecutor)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n执行器实现 (ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n约束算法基类 (ConstraintAlgImpl)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\nCP模型封装 (AlgCPModel)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\nOrtools SAT求解器\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2.2 核心组件\\\\\\\\n- **ModuleConstraintExecutor**: 北向接口，提供统一的约束求解服务\\\\\\\\n- **ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl**: 执行器实现，管理模块和求解流程\\\\\\\\n- **ConstraintAlgImpl**: 约束算法基类，提供南向扩展接口\\\\\\\\n- **AlgCPModel**: CP模型封装，简化Ortools API调用\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. 详细设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3.1 ModuleConstraintExecutor设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.1.1 核心职责\\\\\\\\n- 提供全局单例访问点\\\\\\\\n- 封装内部实现细节\\\\\\\\n- 提供统一的约束求解接口\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.1.2 主要接口\\\\\\\\n- &#96;init(ConstraintConfig)&#96;: 初始化执行器\\\\\\\\n- &#96;addModule(Long, Module...)&#96;: 添加约束模块\\\\\\\\n- &#96;inferParas(InferParasReq)&#96;: 执行参数推理\\\\\\\\n- &#96;registerExtensible(ExtensibleProcess)&#96;: 注册扩展处理器\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.1.3 使用示例\\\\\\\\n以ModuleConstraintExecutorTest.testInitAddModuleAndInfer为例：\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\\\\\\\\n// 1. 初始化执行器\\\\\\\\nModuleConstraintExecutorImpl exec = new ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl();\\\\\\\\nConstraintConfig cfg = new ConstraintConfig();\\\\\\\\ncfg.isAttachedDebug = true;\\\\\\\\nexec.init(cfg);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n// 2. 加载模块\\\\\\\\nModule m = ModuleUtils.fromJsonFile(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"tshirtdata.json\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\nm.init();\\\\\\\\nexec.addModule(123123L, m);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n// 3. 执行参数推理\\\\\\\\nInferParasReq req = new InferParasReq();\\\\\\\\nreq.moduleId = 123123L;\\\\\\\\nreq.enumerateAllSolution = true;\\\\\\\\nResult\u003cList\u003cModuleInst\u003e\u003e result = exec.inferParas(req);\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3.2 ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.2.1 核心职责\\\\\\\\n- 管理模块生命周期\\\\\\\\n- 协调约束求解流程\\\\\\\\n- 处理差量加载和全量加载\\\\\\\\n- 管理扩展处理器\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.2.2 差量加载机制\\\\\\\\n- 根据输入参数确定需要加载的规则和变量\\\\\\\\n- 通过图调度机制查询依赖关系\\\\\\\\n- 只加载必要的约束和变量，提高性能\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.2.3 求解流程\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n接收推理请求\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n查找目标模块\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n创建约束算法实例\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n根据loadType选择加载模式\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n初始化约束模型\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n添加输入约束\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n执行约束求解\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n后处理结果\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n返回解决方案\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3.3 ConstraintAlgImpl设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.3.1 核心职责\\\\\\\\n- 提供约束算法的南向扩展接口\\\\\\\\n- 管理变量创建和约束定义\\\\\\\\n- 支持多种约束类型\\\\\\\\n- 提供规则方法执行机制\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.3.2 南向扩展机制\\\\\\\\n以HelloConstraint为例说明算法扩展：\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\\\\\\\\n@ModuleAnno(id = 123L)\\\\\\\\npublic class HelloConstraint extends ConstraintAlgImpl {\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    @ParaAnno(options = {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Red:1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Black:2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"White:3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"})\\\\\\\\n    private ParaVar colorVar;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    @ParaAnno(options = {\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Small\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Medium\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Big\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"})\\\\\\\\n    private ParaVar sizeVar;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    @PartAnno(price = 100L)\\\\\\\\n    private PartVar tShirt11Var;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    @Override\\\\\\\\n    protected void initConstraint() {\\\\\\\\n        addConstraint_rule2();\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    public void addConstraint_rule2() {\\\\\\\\n        // 实现具体的约束逻辑\\\\\\\\n        // 如果颜色是红色且尺寸是小号，则TShirt11数量为1；否则为3\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.3.3 基于约束求解的反推参数的设计文档\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. 概述\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1.1 设计目标\\\\\\\\n设计一个基于约束求解的反推参数系统，能够根据已知的部件数量和参数值，通过约束求解技术反推出其他未知参数的值。该系统采用图调度机制实现差量加载，支持多种约束类型和复杂的业务规则。\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1.2 初始化约束执行器并添加模块\\\\\\\\n2. 构建约束模型和变量映射\\\\\\\\n3. 通过图调度机制确定需要加载的规则和变量\\\\\\\\n4. 执行约束求解获取参数反推结果\\\\\\\\n5. 后处理优化和结果验证\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. 架构设计\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2.1 整体架构\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n北向接口 (ModuleConstraintExecutor)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n执行器实现 (ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl)\\\\\\\\n    ↓\\\\\\\\n约束算法基类 (ConstraintAlgImpl)\\\\\\\\n 注册扩展处理器\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n#### 3.1.3 使用示例\\\\\\\\n以ModuleConstraintExecutorTest.testInitAddModuleAndInfer为例：\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\\\\\\\\n// 1. com.jmix.scenario.ruletest;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.coretest.ConstraintAlgImplTestBase;\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.coretest.ModuleScenarioTestBase;\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.executor.model.ConstraintConfig;\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.CodeRuleAnno;\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.ModuleAnno;\\\\\\\\nimport com.jmix.tool.bbuilder.anno.ParaAnno;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport com.google.ortools.sat.BoolVar;\\\\\\\\nimport com.google.ortools.sat.Literal;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nimport java.util.Arrays;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n/**\\\\\\\\n * 兼容性规则测试类\\\\\\\\n * \\\\\\\\n * @since 2025-09-23\\\\\\\\n */\\\\\\\\n@Slf4j\\\\\\\\npublic class CompatibleRuleRequireTest extends ModuleScenarioTestBase {\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 构造CompatibleRuleRequireTest测试类\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    public CompatibleRuleRequireTest() {\\\\\\\\n        super(CompatibleRuleRequireConstraint.class);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // ---------------规则定义start----------------------------------------\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 兼容性规则要求约束模型类\\\\\\\\n     * \\\\\\\\n     * @since 2025-09-23\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    @ModuleAnno(id = 123L)\\\\\\\\n    public static class CompatibleRuleRequireConstraint extends ConstraintAlgImplTestBase {\\\\\\\\n        @ParaAnno(options = { \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" })\\\\\\\\n        private ParaVar aVar;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        @ParaAnno(options = { \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b4\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" })\\\\\\\\n        private ParaVar bVar;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        @CodeRuleAnno\\\\\\\\n        private void initConstraint() {\\\\\\\\n            // natural code: aVar.value in (a1,a3) Requires bVar.value in (b1,b2,b3)\\\\\\\\n            // A=(a1,a2,a3,a4)\\\\\\\\n            // B=(b1,b2,b3,b4)\\\\\\\\n            // 规则内容：(a1,a3) Requires (b1,b2,b3)，则CA=(a1,a3),CB=(b1,b2,b3)\\\\\\\\n            // 解读：\\\\\\\\n            // 1、正向\\\\\\\\n            // 1.1 在CA中，如果aVar.var=a1,则bVar.var=b1 或 b2 或 b3\\\\\\\\n            // 1.2 不在CA中，如果aVar.var=a1,则bVar.var=b4，是不合法的\\\\\\\\n            // 2.反向\\\\\\\\n            // 2.1 在CB中，如果bVar.var=b1,则aVar.var=a1 或 a2 或 a3 或 a4\\\\\\\\n            // 2.2 不在CB中，如果bVar.var=b4,则aVar.var=a2 或 a4\\\\\\\\n            addCompatibleConstraintRequires(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"rule1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", aVar, Arrays.asList(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"), bVar,\\\\\\\\n                    Arrays.asList(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"));\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        /**\\\\\\\\n         * 初始化约束2,参考\\\\\\\\n         */\\\\\\\\n        protected void initConstraintNote() {\\\\\\\\n            // 创建条件变量：A是a1或a3\\\\\\\\n            BoolVar a1OrA3 = model.newBoolVar(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1OrA3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            model.addBoolOr(new Literal[] {\\\\\\\\n                    aVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar(),\\\\\\\\n                    aVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar()\\\\\\\\n            }).onlyEnforceIf(a1OrA3);\\\\\\\\n            model.addBoolAnd(new Literal[] {\\\\\\\\n                    aVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar().not(),\\\\\\\\n                    aVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar().not()\\\\\\\\n            }).onlyEnforceIf(a1OrA3.not());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            // 创建条件变量：B是b1、b2或b3\\\\\\\\n            BoolVar b1OrB2OrB3 = model.newBoolVar(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b1OrB2OrB3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n            model.addBoolOr(new Literal[] {\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar(),\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar(),\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar()\\\\\\\\n            }).onlyEnforceIf(b1OrB2OrB3);\\\\\\\\n            model.addBoolAnd(new Literal[] {\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar().not(),\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b2\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar().not(),\\\\\\\\n                    bVar.getParaOptionByCode(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"b3\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\").getIsSelectedVar().not()\\\\\\\\n            }).onlyEnforceIf(b1OrB2OrB3.not());\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            // 添加约束：如果A是a1或a3，则B必须是b1、b2或b3\\\\\\\\n            model.addImplication(a1OrA3, b1OrB2OrB3);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n            // 注意：反向约束不需要显式添加，因为这是\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Requires\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"关系的单向约束\\\\\\\\n            // 反向推理的结果是正向约束的自然结果\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    // ---------------规则定义end----------------------------------------\\\\\\\\n    @Override\\\\\\\\n    protected void beforeInitConfig(ConstraintConfig cfg) {\\\\\\\\n        cfg.setLoadType(ConstraintConfig.LOAD_TYPE_FULL);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    /**\\\\\\\\n     * 测试A1要求B1、B2、B3\\\\\\\\n     */\\\\\\\\n    @Test\\\\\\\\n    public void testA1RequiresB1B2B3() {\\\\\\\\n        // 测试正向规则1.1: 如果A选择a1，则B必须是b1、b2或b3\\\\\\\\n        inferParasByPara(\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"a1\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\");\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n        // B有3种选择\\\\\\\\n        resultAssert()\\\\\\\\n                .assertSuccess()\\\\\\\\n ,\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommands\\\":[],\\\"cursorCommandsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"pastChats\\\":[],\\\"pastChatsExplicitlySet\\\":false,\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-04-26T22:47:23.868Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\u003cthink\u003e\\\\n我已经收集了足够的信息来回答用户的问题。让我整理一下关于这个Java项目的核心代码结构的发现。\\\\n\\\\n主要发现：\\\\n\\\\n1. **项目概述**：这是一个基于约束求解的参数反推系统，使用Google OR-Tools SAT求解器\\\\n\\\\n2. **主要模块**：\\\\n   - &#96;executor&#96; - 核心执行器\\\\n   - &#96;bmodel&#96; - 业务模型\\\\n   - &#96;cmodel&#96; - 约束模型\\\\n   - &#96;southinf&#96; - 南向接口\\\\n   - &#96;tool&#96; - 工具类\\\\n\\\\n3. **核心类**：\\\\n   - &#96;ModuleConstraintExecutorImpl&#96; - 主执行器\\\\n   - &#96;AlgCPModel&#96; - CP-SAT模型封装\\\\n   - &#96;ModuleAlgImpl&#96; - 算法实现\\\\n   - &#96;PartCategory&#96; - 部件分类\\\\n   - &#96;Rule&#96; - 规则定义\\\\n\\\\n4. **技术栈**：\\\\n   - Ortools SAT (v9.12.4544)\\\\n   - Spring AI\\\\n   - FreeMarker\\\\n   - Jackson\\\\n   - Lombok\\\\n\\\\n5. **规则类型**：\\\\n   - 兼容性规则 (CompatiableRule)\\\\n   - 计算规则 (CalculateRule)\\\\n   - 选择规则 (SelectRule)\\\\n   - 优先级规则 (PriorityRule)\\\\n\\\\n现在让我提供完整的回答。\\\\n\u003c/think\u003e\\\\n\\\\n根据我对代码的深入探索，下面是关于这个Java项目核心代码结构的详细分析报告：\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 项目概述\\\\n\\\\n**JMix Config Engine** 是一个基于约束求解的参数反推系统，核心功能是根据已知的部件数量和参数值，通过约束求解技术反推出其他未知参数的值。\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 主要目录结构\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\nsrc/main/java/com/jmix/\\\\n├── executor/                           #
